message:
feat(layout,parser,render-svg,render-term,render-canvas): enhance sequence lifecycle and plan Sankey rendering

- Refine sequence lifecycle marker positioning and coalescing
- Improve parser handling of sequence diagram constructs
- Update SVG, terminal, and canvas renderers for lifecycle markers
- Create beads tracking tasks for Sankey diagram rendering work

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-ct6f","title":"Sankey: Implement flow color gradients and value-proportional rendering","description":"# Sankey Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Flow bands should have color gradients (source color → target color)
- Flow width should be proportional to value (currently approximated)
- Node labels should be positioned outside the flow for readability
- Interactive hover highlighting (WASM only)

## Implementation Plan
1. Implement gradient fills on flow bands using linearGradient defs
2. Ensure flow band width exactly proportional to value/total_flow
3. Position node labels to the left of first-column nodes and right of last-column nodes
4. Add data attributes for WASM hover interaction","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:10.808759311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:31.619408492Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","render","sankey"],"comments":[{"id":162,"issue_id":"bd-ct6f","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:42Z"},{"id":181,"issue_id":"bd-ct6f","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. Two-node sankey with value 100 → verify flow band width proportional to value
2. Three-column sankey (A→B→C) → verify gradient fill on flow bands
3. Node labels → verify left-side labels for first column, right-side for last
4. Multiple flows from same source → verify flows don't overlap
5. Large value disparity (1 vs 1000) → verify thin flow is still visible
6. Self-loop flow (A→A) → verify graceful handling","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-dcjc","title":"Polyhedral optimization for layout algorithm loop nests","description":"Apply polyhedral optimization (graveyard section 6.5) to crossing minimization and coordinate assignment inner loops. Cache-optimal traversal patterns.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:04.637547948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:04.637547948Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","frankenmermaid","layout"]}
 {"id":"bd-drav","title":"Parse and render sequence diagram interaction fragments (loop/alt/opt/par/critical/break)","description":"# Sequence Interaction Fragments

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  Alice->>Bob: Request
  loop Every minute
    Bob->>Alice: Heartbeat
  end
  alt Success
    Bob->>Alice: 200 OK
  else Failure
    Bob->>Alice: 500 Error
  end
  opt Optional
    Alice->>Carol: Forward
  end
  par Parallel
    Alice->>Bob: Task 1
  and
    Alice->>Carol: Task 2
  end
  critical Critical Section
    Alice->>Bob: Important
  option Timeout
    Alice->>Bob: Retry
  end
  break When error
    Alice->>Bob: Abort
  end
  rect rgb(200, 220, 240)
    Alice->>Bob: Highlighted
  end
\`\`\`

Fragment types: loop, alt/else, opt, par/and, critical/option, break, rect (colored background).
Each fragment is a labeled box spanning the involved participants, enclosing the messages within.
Fragments can be nested.

## Current State
- NOT parsed. Fragment keywords produce warnings.
- No layout support for fragment regions.
- No SVG rendering of fragment boxes.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Add SequenceStatement::FragmentStart { kind, label }, FragmentElse { label }, FragmentEnd. Track nesting depth. Kind enum: Loop, Alt, Opt, Par, Critical, Break, Rect.
2. **IR**: Store as Vec<IrSequenceFragment> with kind, label, start_message_index, end_message_index, participant_range, and nested children.
3. **Layout**: Fragments add padding around their contained messages. Compute fragment box bounds from the first/last message y-positions and the leftmost/rightmost participant x-positions. Nested fragments get progressively larger bounds.
4. **SVG Renderer**: Draw fragment boxes as dashed-border rectangles with a label tab in the top-left corner. Fragment kind shown in bold in the tab. 'else'/'and'/'option' dividers shown as dashed horizontal lines within the fragment.
5. **Tests**: loop, alt/else, opt, par/and, nested fragments, rect with color.

## Complexity
This is the single most complex sequence diagram feature. Fragment nesting requires a stack-based parser and recursive layout. Budget 2-3x the effort of a simple task.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.719718813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:25:34.360084926Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:25:34.359593546Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence interaction fragment parsing (loop, alt, opt, par, critical, break, rect). Stack-based parser tracks nesting depth. Fragments store kind, label, start/end edge indices, alternatives (for else/and/option dividers), and nested child indices. 'end' keyword dispatches to close either fragments or box groups. 6 tests added covering all fragment types, alt/else alternatives, nested fragments, rect with color, and warning-free parsing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity","parser","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-drav","depends_on_id":"bd-6zd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:25.838352205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-drav","depends_on_id":"bd-7g6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:29.797041627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":146,"issue_id":"bd-drav","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests

- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets

- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":175,"issue_id":"bd-drav","author":"ubuntu","text":"SCOPE NOTE: This bead is intentionally large because the fragment parser, layout integration, and renderer are tightly coupled. Splitting parser from renderer would create a half-baked intermediate state (parsed but invisible fragments). Implementors should budget 2-3x normal effort. Suggested approach: implement loop first (simplest fragment), then alt/else (adds alternation), then the rest. Each fragment type can be tested incrementally.

DETAILED TEST CASES:
1. Simple loop: loop Every minute / Bob->>Alice: ping / end → verify dashed box with 'loop' tab, contains 1 message
2. Alt with else: alt OK / A->>B: 200 / else Error / A->>B: 500 / end → verify two sections separated by dashed line
3. Nested: loop / alt / A->>B: x / else / A->>B: y / end / end → verify nested boxes with correct containment
4. Par with and: par / A->>B: t1 / and / A->>C: t2 / end → verify parallel sections
5. Rect with color: rect rgb(200,200,240) / A->>B: x / end → verify colored background
6. Empty fragment: loop Empty / end → verify empty box renders without crash
7. Fragment spanning all participants: verify box width covers leftmost to rightmost participant involved
8. 3-level nesting: loop / critical / opt / A->>B / end / end / end → verify 3 nested boxes","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:30Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-dsjl","title":"Sequence: implement rect background highlighting parity","description":"# Sequence rect highlighting parity

## Goal
Make Mermaid sequence `rect COLOR ... end` fragments render as actual background highlighting instead of a generic fragment box.

## Scope
- Preserve the rect color distinctly in sequence IR and layout extensions.
- Render SVG rect fragments with the configured fill color and a sensible border, while keeping unsafe paint values out of the output.
- Keep non-rect fragments on existing generic fragment rendering.
- Add focused parser/layout/render tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `rect rgb(...)` and `rect rgba(...)` render as highlighted sequence regions in SVG.
- `rect transparent` renders without an opaque fill.
- Existing loop/alt/opt/par/critical/break fragment rendering remains intact.
- Required cargo checks pass via `rch` and formatting is clean.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T21:15:29.112583706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T21:15:38.943831609Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-e1sg","title":"Implement sequence participant aliases and box grouping","description":"# Sequence Participant Aliases and Box Grouping

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  participant A as Alice
  actor B as Bob
  box Blue Team
    participant A
    participant B
  end
  A->>B: Hello
\`\`\`
- Participant aliases: \`participant shortName as Display Name\`
- Box grouping: groups participants visually in a colored box with label
- create/destroy: \`create participant C\` and \`destroy C\`

## Current State
- Alias syntax (\`as\`) IS parsed for participant declarations
- Box grouping is NOT parsed
- create/destroy are NOT parsed

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse \`box [color] Label ... end\` blocks. Parse \`create participant\` and \`destroy Name\`.
2. **IR**: Add participant groups and lifecycle events to IrSequenceMeta.
3. **Layout**: Group boxes add horizontal containment around grouped participants. Destroyed participants have shortened lifelines.
4. **SVG Renderer**: Draw participant group boxes. Draw destruction X on lifeline.
5. **Tests**: Alias rendering, box grouping, create/destroy lifecycle.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:10.055763292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:16:15.741638433Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:16:15.741110364Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence participant box grouping and lifecycle events. Parser handles 'box [color] Label...end' blocks, 'create participant', 'create actor', and 'destroy Name'. Participant groups tracked in IrBuilder with auto-close on diagram end. 5 tests added covering box grouping with/without color, create/destroy events, and warning-free parsing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-e1sg","depends_on_id":"bd-6zd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:27.407572780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":148,"issue_id":"bd-e1sg","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":202,"issue_id":"bd-e1sg","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL: mermaid-js also supports a 'links' keyword for attaching URLs to participants:
```

  participant A as Alice
  links A: {"Dashboard": "https://...", "Wiki": "https://..."}
```
This renders as small link icons or a dropdown on the participant header. Parser should recognize 'links ParticipantName: {JSON}' and store URLs on the participant node.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-y6o8","title":"Implement style and linkStyle directive parsing and rendering","description":"# style / linkStyle Directive Support

## What mermaid-js Supports
Inline styling for specific nodes and edges:
\`\`\`
style nodeId fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:4px
linkStyle 0 stroke:#ff3,stroke-width:4px,color:red
linkStyle default stroke:#333
\`\`\`

style applies to a node by ID. linkStyle applies to an edge by index (0-based declaration order) or 'default' for all edges.

## Current State
- Parser recognizes style/linkStyle lines but does not capture the property map
- No rendering path for inline styles

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse \`style nodeId prop:value,...\` and store as IrNodeStyle on the IrNode. Parse \`linkStyle index prop:value,...\` and store as IrEdgeStyle on the IrEdge.
2. **IR**: Add \`inline_style: Option<IrInlineStyle>\` to IrNode and IrEdge. IrInlineStyle is a BTreeMap<String, String>.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Apply inline_style as SVG attributes (fill → rect fill, stroke → rect stroke, etc.). For edges, apply to <path> elements.
4. **Tests**: Verify style is applied in SVG output, verify linkStyle by index, verify default linkStyle.

## Considerations
- Property names must be mapped from CSS-like (stroke-width) to SVG attributes
- Sanitize values to prevent XSS in SVG output","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.486402415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23.800287285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-y6o8","depends_on_id":"bd-77gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:23.246993348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":140,"issue_id":"bd-y6o8","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"},{"id":185,"issue_id":"bd-y6o8","author":"ubuntu","text":"CROSS-DIAGRAM NOTE: style/linkStyle directives apply to flowchart primarily but the IR infrastructure should be generic enough for any diagram type. Also: style supports comma-separated node IDs: 'style nodeA,nodeB,nodeC fill:#f9f' applies to multiple nodes at once. The parser must split on commas and apply the style to each node.","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-yh4v","title":"Implement gantt date parsing, task types, and duration computation","description":"# Gantt Date/Duration/Task Type Parsing

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
gantt
  dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
  title Project Plan
  excludes weekends
  section Phase 1
  Design           :done, des1, 2024-01-01, 30d
  Implementation   :active, impl1, after des1, 60d
  Testing          :crit, test1, after impl1, 2024-05-01
  Milestone        :milestone, m1, after test1, 0d
\`\`\`
- dateFormat: YYYY-MM-DD, DD/MM/YYYY, etc.
- Task types: done, active, crit (critical), milestone
- Duration: Nd (days), after taskId (dependency), absolute dates
- excludes: weekends, specific dates

## Current State
- Parser recognizes sections and task lines, but does NOT extract dates, types, durations, or dependencies
- Layout has a gantt algorithm but operates on generic nodes, not time-aware bars

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: After the colon in task lines, parse comma-separated metadata: type (done/active/crit/milestone), id, start (date or 'after id'), end (date or duration). Parse dateFormat directive. Parse excludes.
2. **IR**: Add IrGanttMeta { date_format, excludes, sections: Vec<IrGanttSection> } with IrGanttTask { id, name, task_type, start, end, depends_on }.
3. **Layout**: Compute actual x-positions from dates. Apply excludes to skip weekends. Position milestone as diamonds.
4. **Tests**: Date formats, durations, after-dependencies, excludes, milestones, crit tasks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:07.294100500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31.708821785Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gantt","parity","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","depends_on_id":"bd-0bu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:28.205838996Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":155,"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41Z"},{"id":196,"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL GANTT DIRECTIVES TO PARSE:
- todayMarker stroke-width:5px,stroke:#0f0,opacity:0.5 → custom styling for the today line. Store as optional style string in IrGanttMeta.
- tickInterval 1day / 1week / 1month → controls axis label granularity. Store as enum (Day/Week/Month/Quarter/Year) in IrGanttMeta.
- inclusiveEndDates → when true, task end dates include the end day. Store as bool in IrGanttMeta.
- weekday monday → sets start of week. Store as optional u8 in IrGanttMeta.
- axisFormat %Y-%m-%d → date format for axis labels. Store as String in IrGanttMeta.

These must ALL be parsed for full parity. Update the IrGanttMeta type definition in bd-0bu2 accordingly.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31Z"}]}

+{"id":"bd-zi0e","title":"Sequence: support named box colors and transparent box syntax parity","description":"# Sequence box color parity

## Goal
Support Mermaid sequence box declarations that use named colors and the special transparent form, matching current docs examples like `box Aqua Group Description` and `box transparent Aqua`.

## Scope
- Parse leading named CSS color tokens in sequence `box` declarations, not just hex/rgb/hsl forms.
- Treat `box transparent <label>` as transparent box fill with `<label>` preserved as the label.
- Preserve the parsed group color through sequence IR/layout to SVG rendering for participant group clusters.
- Add focused parser/layout/render tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `box Aqua Group` stores color `Aqua` and label `Group`.
- `box transparent Aqua` stores a transparent color and label `Aqua`.
- SVG uses the parsed group color without unsafe attribute injection.
- Quality gates for touched crates pass via `rch` and formatting is clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:41:43.328437882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T20:48:15.194234898Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T20:48:15.193746754Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence box color parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-zmbp","title":"State: Complete feature parity with mermaid-js","description":"# State Diagram Feature Parity

## Current State
Basic states and transitions work (~75% parser). Missing all UML pseudostate types that make state diagrams expressive.

## Gaps
1. Start/end pseudo-state special rendering ([*])
2. Fork/join parallel bars
3. Choice pseudo-state (diamond shape)
4. History states (H / H*)
5. Composite/nested states with proper containment
6. Notes on states
7. Entry/exit actions on transitions
8. Guard conditions [guard] on transitions
9. State descriptions (state name : description)
10. Concurrency regions (-- separator within composite state)

 {"id":"bd-zn9n","title":"Implement composite/nested states with containment and concurrency","description":"# Composite/Nested States and Concurrency

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
stateDiagram-v2
  state Active {
    [*] --> Processing
    Processing --> Done
    --
    [*] --> Monitoring
    Monitoring --> Alert
  }
  Idle --> Active
  Active --> Idle
\`\`\`
- States can contain child states (composite state)
- \`--\` separator creates concurrent regions within a composite state
- Each concurrent region has independent start/end states
- Nesting can be arbitrarily deep

## Current State
- Composite state syntax (\`state Name { ... }\`) IS parsed — creates subgraphs
- Concurrency separator \`--\` is NOT parsed
- Layout does not enforce visual containment of child states
- SVG renderer draws clusters but doesn't render concurrency dividers

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse \`--\` within composite state blocks as region separators. Each region becomes a separate sub-cluster within the parent cluster.
2. **Layout**: Ensure cluster bounds fully contain all child node bounds. For concurrent regions, split the cluster horizontally with a dashed divider.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Draw composite states with rounded rectangle borders. Draw dashed horizontal line for concurrency dividers. Add region labels if present.
4. **Tests**: Nested states, three levels deep, concurrent regions, transitions crossing region boundaries.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:45:20.201073543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T19:12:15.275149600Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T19:12:15.274637351Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity","parser","render","state"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zn9n","depends_on_id":"bd-2gwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:31.377491237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":153,"issue_id":"bd-zn9n","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)

+fn sanitize_svg_paint(value: &str) -> Option<String> {
+    let trimmed = value.trim();
+    if trimmed.is_empty() {
+        return None;
+    }
+
+    let lower = trimmed.to_ascii_lowercase();
+    if is_css_named_color(&lower) {
+        return Some(lower);
+    }
+
+    if trimmed.starts_with('#')
+        && trimmed[1..].chars().all(|ch| ch.is_ascii_hexdigit())
+        && matches!(trimmed.len(), 4 | 5 | 7 | 9)
+    {
+        return Some(trimmed.to_string());
+    }
+
+    for prefix in ["rgb(", "rgba(", "hsl(", "hsla("] {
+        if lower.starts_with(prefix)
+            && lower.ends_with(')')
+            && trimmed.chars().all(|ch| {
+                ch.is_ascii_alphanumeric()
+                    || matches!(ch, '(' | ')' | ',' | '.' | '%' | '/' | ' ' | '+' | '-')
+            })
+        {
+            return Some(trimmed.to_string());
+        }
+    }
+
+    None
+}
+
